summary:
Claim: Erythrocytes are anucleate and lack MHC antigens. 
Evidence: - Red blood cells (RBCs) lose their nucleus during maturation, preventing MHC gene expression. - Most nucleated cells express MHC class I, except specialized cases like placental trophoblasts. 
Answer: B
formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): Claim: Erythrocytes are anucleate and lack MHC antigens. 
Evidence: - Red blood cells (RBCs) lose their nucleus during maturation, preventing MHC gene expression. - Most nucleated cells express MHC class I, except specialized cases like placental trophoblasts. 
Answer: B. Answer choice: B
